message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first capture relay enqueue (tick 267)

Migrates the capture-relay task's SPSC enqueue (runtime.rs L2765) to
the `send_with_cx` sibling on `SpscProducer`:

- `capture_ring_tx.send(event)` →
  `send_with_cx(&relay_cx, event)`

The capture relay is the sole producer for the persistence SPSC ring,
draining the bounded MPSC ingress channel. Under asupersync this is a
hot-path loop polling `recv_event` with a 25ms timeout and forwarding
each event to the SPSC ring consumed by the persistence worker.

Prior to this tick, the SPSC enqueue was cx-insensitive — a future
operator-level cancel propagating into the relay task would have to
wait for the send to complete (with backpressure blocking against the
ring) before the task could observe cancellation. The `_with_cx`
variant (spsc_ring_buffer.rs L139) gates the send on `cx.checkpoint()`
so cx-cancel aborts the enqueue cleanly without waiting for ring
capacity.

Cx resolved via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)` inside the
spawned task body. Spawned relay doesn't thread Cx through its
`task::spawn(async move { ... })` — the current cx on the worker thread
is picked up per-iteration.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 81 (187-267).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 45 (runtime 11 + mcp 2 + tui 8 + web 4
+ mcp_helpers 2 + mcp_tools 10 + workflows/runner 1
+ workflows/account_steps 1 + tx_execution 3 + simulation 1
+ workflows/wait_execution 2 + wezterm trait ext 1 + recording 2).
main.rs cross-crate total: 127 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
